author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CXLIV

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The title of this Psalm is brief in number of words, but heavy in the weight of its mysteries.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To David himself against Goliath.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This battle was fought in the time of our fathers, and ye, beloved, remember it with me from Holy Scripture.…David put five stones in his scrip, he hurled but one.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The five Books were chosen, but unity conquered.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then, having smitten and overthrown him, he took the enemy’s sword, and with it cut off his head.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This our David also did, He overthrew the devil with his own weapons: and when his great ones, whom he had in his power, by means of whom he slew other souls, believe, they turn their tongues against the devil, and so Goliath’s head is cut off with his own sword.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Blessed be the Lord my God, who teacheth my hands for battle, my fingers for war” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are our words, if we be the Body of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It seems a repetition of sentiment; “our hands for battle,” and “our fingers for war,” are the same.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Or is there some difference between “hands” and “fingers”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Certainly both hands and fingers work.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not then without reason do we take “fingers” as put for “hands.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But still in the “fingers” we recognise the division of operation, yet still a sort of unity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold that grace! the Apostle saith, To one, this; to another, that; “there are diversities of operations; all these worketh one and the self-same Spirit;” there is the root of unity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With these “fingers” then the Body of Christ fighteth, going forth to “war,” going forth to “battle.”…By works of Mercy our enemy is conquered, and we could not have works of mercy unless we had charity, and charity we could have none unless we received it by the Holy Ghost; He then “teacheth our hands for battle, and our fingers for war:” to Him rightfully do we say, “My Mercy,” from whom we have also that we are merciful: “for he shall have judgment without mercy, that hath showed no mercy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: My Mercy and my Refuge, my Upholder and my Deliverer” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Much toileth this combatant, having his flesh lusting against his spirit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Keep what thou hast.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then shalt thou have in full what thou wishest, when “death shall have been swallowed up in victory;” when this mortal body has been raised, and is changed into the condition of the angels, and rises aloft to a heavenly quality.…There is life, there are good days, where nought lusteth against the spirit, where it is not said, “Fight,” but “Rejoice.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But who is he that lusteth for these days?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Every man certainly saith, “I do.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear what followeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I see that thou art toiling, I see that thou art engaged in battle, and in danger; hear what followeth:…“Depart from evil, and do good:” let not the poor first weep under thee, that the poor may rejoice through thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what reward, since now thou art fighting?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Seek peace, and ensue it.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Learn and say, “My Mercy and my Refuge, mine Upholder and my Deliverer, my Protector:” “mine Upholder,” lest I fall; “my Deliverer,” lest I stick; “my Protector,” lest I be stricken.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In all these things, in all my toil, in all my battles, in all my difficulties, in Him have I hoped, “who subdueth my people under me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, our Head speaketh together with us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lord, what is man, that Thou hast become known unto him?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All is included in “that Thou hast become known unto him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Or the son of man, that Thou valuest him?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou valuest him, that is, Thou makest him of such importance, Thou countest him of such price, Thou knowest under what Thou placest him, over what Thou placest him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For valuing is considering the price of a thing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How greatly did He value man, who for him shed the blood of His only-begotten Son!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For God valueth not man in the same way as one man valueth another: he, when he findeth a slave for sale, giveth a higher price for a horse than for a man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Consider how greatly He valued thee, that thou mayest be able to say, “If God be for us, who can be against us?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And how greatly did He value thee, “who spared not His own Son”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “How shall He not also with Him freely give us all things?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who giveth this food to the combatant, what keepeth He in store for the conqueror?…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Man is made like unto vanity: his days pass away like a shadow” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What vanity?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Time, which passeth on, and floweth by.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this “vanity” is said in comparison of the Truth, which ever abideth, and never faileth: for it too is a work of His Hand, in its degree.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For,” as it is written, “God filled the earth with His good things.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is “His”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That accord with Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But all these things, being earthly, fleeting, transitory, if they be compared to that Truth, where it is said, “I Am That I Am,” all this which passeth away is called “vanity.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For through time it vanisheth, like stroke into the air.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And why should I say more than that which the Apostle James said, willing to bring down proud men to humility, “What is,” saith he, “your life?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is even a vapour, which appeareth for a little time, and then vanisheth away.”…Work then, though it be in the night, with thine hands, that is, by good works seek God, before the day come which shall gladden thee, lest the day come which shall sadden thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For see how safely thou workest, who art not left by Him whom thou seekest; “that thy Father which seeth in secret may reward thee openly.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lord, bow Thy heavens, and come down: touch the mountains, and they shall smoke” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Flash Thy lightning, and Thou shalt scatter them; send forth Thine arrows, and Thou shalt confound them” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Send forth Thy Hand from above, and deliver me, and draw me out of many waters” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Body of Christ, the humble David, full of grace, relying on God, fighting in this world, calleth for the help of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are “heavens bowed down”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Apostles humbled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For those “heavens declare the glory of God;” and of these heavens declaring the glory of God it is presently said, “There is neither speech nor language, but their voices are heard among them,” etc. When then these heavens sent forth their voices through all lands, and did wonderful things, while the Lord flashed and thundered from them by miracles and commandments, the gods were thought to have come down from heaven to men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For certain of the Gentiles, thinking this, desired even to sacrifice to them.…But they commended to these the Lord Jesus Christ, humbling themselves, that God might be praised; because “the heavens” were “bowed,” that “God” might “come down.”…“Touch the mountains, and they shall smoke.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So long as they are not touched, they seem to themselves great: they are now about to say, “Great art Thou, O Lord:” the mountains also are about to say, “Thou only art the Most Highest over all the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But there are some that conspire, that “gather themselves together against the Lord, and against His Christ.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have come together, they have conspired.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Flash forth Thy lightnings, and Thou shalt scatter them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Abound with Thy miracles, and their conspiracy shall be broken.…“Send forth Thine arrows, and Thou shalt confound them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the unsound be wounded, that, being well wounded, they may be made sound; and let them say, being set now in the Church, in the Body of Christ, let them say with the Church, “I am wounded with Love.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Send forth Thine Hand from on high.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What afterward?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What in the end?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How conquereth the Body of Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By heavenly aid.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the Lord Himself shall come with the voice of the Archangel, and with the trump of God shall He descend from heaven,” Himself the Saviour of the body, the Hand of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “Out of many waters”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From many peoples.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What peoples?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Aliens, unbelievers, whether assailing us from without, or laying snares within.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Take me out of many waters, in which Thou didst discipline me, in which Thou didst roll me, to free me from my filth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the “water of contradiction.”…“From the hand of strange children.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear, brethren, among whom we are, among whom we live, from whom we long to be delivered.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Whose mouth hath spoken vanity” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All of you to-day, if ye had not gathered yourselves together to these divine shows of the word of God, and were not at this hour engaged in them, how great vanities would ye be hearing! “whose mouth hath spoken vanity:” when, in short, would they, speaking vanity, hear you speaking vanity?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And their right hand is a right hand of iniquity.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What doest thou among them with thy pastoral scrip with five stones in it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Say it to me in another form: that same law which thou hast signified by five stones, signify in some other way also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will sing a new song unto Thee, O God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “A new song” is of grace; “a new song” is of the new man; “a new song” is of the New Testament.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But lest thou shouldest think that grace departeth from the law, whereas rather by grace the law is fulfilled, “upon a psaltery of ten strings will I sing unto Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Upon the law of ten commandments: therein may I sing to Thee; therein may I rejoice to Thee; therein may “I sing to Thee a new song;” for, “Love is the fulfilling of the law.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But they who have not love may carry the psaltery, sing they cannot.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Contradiction cannot make my psaltery to be silent.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who giveth salvation to kings, who redeemeth David His servant” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye know who David is; be yourselves David.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence “redeemeth He David His servant”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence redeemeth He Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence redeemeth He the Body of Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “From the sword of ill intent deliver me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “From the sword” is not sufficient; he addeth, “of ill intent.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Without doubt there is a sword of good intent.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is the sword of good intent?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That whereof the Lord saith, “I came not to send peace on earth, but a sword.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He was about to separate believers from unbelievers, sons from parents, and to sever all other ties, while the sword cut off what was diseased, but healed the members of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of good intent then is the sword twice sharpened, powerful with both edges, the Old and New Testaments, with the narration of the past and the promise of the future.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That then is the sword of good intent: but the other is of ill intent, wherewith they talk vanity, for that is of good intent, wherewith God speaketh verity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For truly “the sons of men have teeth which are spears and arrows, and their tongue is a sharp sword.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “From” this “sword deliver me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And take me out of the hand of strange children, whose mouth hath spoken vanity:” just as before.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And that which followeth, “their right hand is a right hand of iniquity,” the same he had set down before also, when he called them “many waters.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For lest thou shouldest think that the “many waters” were good waters, he explained them by the “sword of ill intent.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Whose sons are like young vines firmly planted in their youth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He wisheth to recount their happiness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe, ye sons of light, sons of peace: observe, ye sons of the Church, members of Christ; observe whom he calleth “strangers,” whom he calleth “strange children,” whom he calleth “waters of contradiction,” whom he calleth a “sword of ill intent.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe, I beseech you, for among them ye are in peril, among their tongues ye fight against the desires of your flesh, among their tongues, set in the hand of the devil wherewith he fighteth.…What vanity hath their mouth spoken, and how is their right hand a right hand of iniquity?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Their daughters are fitted and adorned after the similitude of a temple.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Their garners are full, bursting out from one store to another: their sheep are fruitful, multiplying in their streets” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 13): “their oxen are fat: their hedge is not broken down, nor their road, nor is their crying in their streets” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is not this then happiness?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I ask the sons of the kingdom of heaven, I ask the offspring of everlasting resurrection, I ask the body of Christ, the members of Christ, the temple of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is not this then happiness, to have sons safe, daughters beautiful, garners full, cattle abundant, no downfall, I say not of a wall, but not even of a hedge, no tumult and clamour in the streets, but quiet, peace, abundance, plenty of all things in their houses and in their cities?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is not this then happiness? or ought the righteous to shun it? or findest thou not the house of the righteous too abounding with all these things, full of this happiness?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did not Abraham’s house abound with gold, silver, children, servants, cattle?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What say we? is not this happiness?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be it so, still it is on the left hand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, on the left hand?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Temporal, mortal, bodily.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I desire not that thou shun it, but that thou think it not to be on the right hand.…For what ought they to have set on the right hand?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God, eternity, the years of God which fail not, whereof is said, “and Thy years shall not fail.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There should be the right hand, there should be our longing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us use the left for the time, let us long for the fight for eternity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If riches increase, set not your heart upon them.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They have called the people blessed who have these things” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O men that speak vanity!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have lost the true right hand, wicked and perverse, they have put on the benefits of God inversely.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O wicked ones, O speakers of vanity, O strange children!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What was on the left hand, they have set on the right.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What dost thou, David?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What dost thou, Body of Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What do ye, members of Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What do ye, not strange children, but children of God?…What say ye?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Say ye with us, “Blessed is the people whose Lord is their God.”
